work_unit: fix .env parser inline-comment + escaped-quote support (audit L-002)
situation: Derived fix from audit-2026-07-02-008 finding L-002. config.py load_env() used naive strip('"').strip("'") which (a) broke on passwords containing '#' (e.g. 'pass#word' kept as-is but 'val # comment' had no way to express the literal '#'), and (b) couldn't express literal quotes inside quoted values. Users with '#' in API keys or passwords would see silent truncation if they tried to add an inline comment, and there was no escape mechanism for values that themselves contain quote chars.
action: Replaced inline strip logic with dedicated _parse_env_value() helper. Double/single-quoted values now scan for matching unescaped close-quote, preserving '#' and spaces inside; support \" \' \\ escape sequences. Unquoted values treat first ' #' (space+hash) as inline-comment start; trailing whitespace stripped. load_env() docstring expanded. Verified by 12-case unit test + 6-case E2E test covering: simple/trailing-space/inline-comment/hash-without-space/double-quoted/single-quoted/quoted-hash/quoted-space-hash/escaped-dquote/escaped-backslash/empty/setdefault-no-override. All pass.
outcome_evidence: Commit 763f70e pushed to origin/auto/opt-personal-assistant-1782909325. 12/12 unit cases + 6/6 E2E cases pass. opt-worktree.sh classified as route-fix (no public-interface touch), reused existing area worktree.
